prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, my dear, a CPU is like the brain of a computer. It's a little chip that sits inside the computer and does all the thinking and calculations. When you click on something or type, the CPU figures out what to do and tells the rest of the computer how to do it. It's very smart and works really fast, much faster than my old brain these days! I don't fully understand all the technical details, but I know it's a very important part that makes computers work. Does that help explain it a bit?
